steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The passage says the speaker "declares her intention to create art that has a universal appeal across generations." The correct quotation must show the speaker actively stating a plan to make something for people of all ages — not simply describing a need or addressing an unrelated subject.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
Work through each option:
  • Choice A — ‘"Our world, so worn and weary, / Needs music, pure and strong. / To hush the jangle and discords / Of sorrow, pain and wrong." (from "*Songs for the People*")’: Describes society's need for music but contains no declaration by the speaker of her own artistic intention. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘"Let me make the songs for the people, / Songs for the old and young: / Songs to stir like a battle-cry / Wherever they are sung." (from "*Songs for the People*")’: The speaker directly declares her intention ("Let me make") to create songs for both old and young — universal, cross-generational appeal stated in her own voice. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘"God bless our native land, / Land of the newly free, / Oh may she ever stand / For truth and liberty," (from "*God Bless Our Native Land*")’: A patriotic address with no reference to artistic creation or generational scope. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘"My mother's kiss, my mother's kiss, / I feel its impress now, / As in the bright and happy days / She pressed it on my brow." (from "*My Mother's Kiss*")’: A personal memory with no connection to universal artistic intent. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
